steps.title: Identify the main verb
steps.content: ”Rejects the confirmationist position” at the sentence's end is the main verb, with no coordinating conjunction to connect it to the blank. This means the blank cannot contain a second finite verb.

steps.title: Use a participle to modify the subject
steps.content: When a sentence has one main clause, modifiers of the subject should be non-finite verb forms (participles), not finite verbs. “Dictating” (present participle) modifies “theory,” working alongside “termed the criterion of falsifiability” to complete the subject, with “rejects” as the main verb. “Dictates,” “was dictating,” and “dictated” are all finite verb forms that would create a comma splice. This matches choice C — C is correct.
